We recommended that the Management: a) provide a report documenting the measures observed in the selection of the recipients and the distribution process; and b) establish a clear-cut guidelines on the selection and evaluation of the recipients in order to ensure that the distribution shall be made in a more equitable manner.

Management Comments:
The LGU submitted a written justification dated April 21, 2025 which was received by this Office on April 29, 2025 and explained that the Municipality created an Executive Order (EO) No. 47-B Series of 2023 dubbed as “An order establishing the guidelines for the identification and selection of sites for installation of jetmatic pumps in the Municipality of Camaligan.”

Accordingly, this EO outlines the framework for equitable distribution of pitcher pumps across different barangays, establishes the purpose, coverage, criteria for identification and selection of project sites, pre-assessment procedures, installation requirements, and effectivity to ensure fair and systematic implementation.

Moreso, the Management also explain that the request was submitted by each applicant and submitted to the Mayor’s Office for further consideration and evaluation.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team acknowledge the management’s immediate actions and justification made together with the submission of documents for further evaluation. We noted, however, that despite the EO they submitted setting the guidelines and procedures for distribution and installation of jetmatic pumps, there were no parameters provided as to the frequency of distribution and how many times the recipient can avail of the free pitcher pumps. This observation was rooted from the fact that the Municipality implemented and distributed jetmatic and pitcher pumps for more than two years already. Likewise, we noted that there was no monitoring being implemented for those pitcher pumps previously installed before another set was distributed within the same barangay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the Accountant to expedite the reversion to the National Treasury the total amount of ₱2,935,078.46 as of November 30, 2021, these accounts are various balances of discontinued programs of the municipality in prior years and are lodged in the Cash in Bank account that remained inactive for years.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately to the Bureau of Treasury the total amount ₱2,935,078.46.
Require the Accountant to merge/consolidate the inactive bank accounts totaling ₱18,732,567.41 with the same purpose as that of the latest/active bank accounts in order to avoid additional cost/bank charges in the keeping and maintaining of these bank accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Bank account with the pertinent Cashbooks and the Cash-in Bank accounts and to make the necessary adjustments thereon.
Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts based on the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) prepared. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to also furnish the Municipal Treasurer copies of the BRS for the recording of reconciling items, if any, pertaining to her records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an aging of accounts receivables, make the final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances and prepare the DVs together with the supporting schedule for the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check payable to the Source Agency.
We further recommended that remaining balances which are considered as special purpose funds should be retained and fund balances corresponding to original releases such as financial assistance or subsidy, as well as funds which the disposition of the unused balance is not specified, maybe reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality for future utilization thereof subject to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ascertain the collectability of the remaining receivables. Management shall exert efforts to collect these receivables by issuing demand letters to the debtors, if these are known.
Should the dormant receivables still remain uncollected despite the diligent efforts exerted to collect the same, the Municipal Accountant shall request for authority to write off the dormant receivables observing the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify the dormant receivables of the LGU and issue demand letters to recipients/grantees and request for authority to write-off, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivables to ensure that these are timely collected when the accounts become due and demandable.

We recommended that the Management increase the bond premium of the Municipal Treasurer within the threshold of her total accountability required in Section 5.1 of the Bureau of Treasury Order No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the accounting records and the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances.
b. Henceforth, require both officials to maintain and regularly update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of the account balances.

We recommended that Management prepare an adjusting entry to remove from the PPE accounts those properties costing less than ₱15,000 and transfer to the appropriate inventory/expense/prior period accounts. Likewise, an Inventory Custodian Slip shall be prepared for properties that have already been issued with unit cost of less than ₱15,000 but which can be used for more than one year.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal of information relative to Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act, to ensure that accountability and transparency in the procurement is upheld.

We recommended that Management require concerned officials to submit copies of Contracts/POs to the Auditor, with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, and the related Notification of Delivery of Services/Supplies and Materials, within 24 hours after acceptance of such deliveries of services/supplies and materials, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular 96-010.

We recommended that Management to require the GSO Officer to monitor and comply strictly with the timely submission of Contracts involving government spaces, market/slaughterhouse stalls leased to government/private individuals pursuant to the above-mentioned regulation.

We recommended that the LCE provide justification why the mandatory requirement for a CPES is not observed.  Henceforth, we enjoin LCE to revisit the provisions as provided under Section 13 of the Annex “E” of the R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR on the evaluation of contractor’s performance.  Further, that concerned department representatives be convened to discuss on the subject matter to ascertain commitment/s in terms of agency resources towards conformance to the mandatory requirement for a CPES and its necessary impact to the operations and structure of the LGU.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), expedite the full establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person continue to develop and implement GAD related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We commended Management for implementing majority of its projects within the same budget year, substantially achieving the desired developmental impact in the community.
Further, we reiterated our previous recommendations that Management:
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineer’s Office to fast track the implementation of the various projects in order to achieve the desired outcomes which are essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives in improving the quality of life of its constituents;
Maximize the utilization of funds allocated for the implementation of various projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development during the year; and
Adopt strategies, despite personnel limitations, so that planned projects by the LGU may be timely implemented within the budget year and completed within the stipulated time frames.
We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC and MSWDO to closely monitor and implement the PPAs for LCPC to further maximize the utilization of the LCPC fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the DepEd representatives to furnish the LSB with the Three-year Strategic Program and Recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS, together with the approved SIP and DEDP, to support the approved CY 2017 Annual LSB SEF Budget and furnish the same to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the LSB to obtain from the DepEd ROV representatives the aforecited Program, Recommendation and approved SIP and DEDP prior to the preparation of the Annual SEF Budget, as guide/basis for the preparation and approval of the Annual SEF Budget by the LSB, and copy furnished the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱4.96 million following the procedures in Sections
8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) request the MSWDO to revisit and follow the Omnibus Guidelines and to:
Provide the Audit Team with the masterlist of beneficiaries of the SFP;
Formulate  profiling  and  data  banking  of  children  as  required  under DSWD Administrative Orders; and
Attach  the  documents  in  the  liquidation  of  the  CAs  for  SFP  and the required Evaluation Report on the Nutritional Status of the children
We also recommended that Management open a bank account solely for the transfer of funds for the DSWD Supplementary Feeding Program as required under the Omnibus Guidelines.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Unit and Property/Supplies Unit to take actions on the following:
Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts;
Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances; and
Maintain complete and accurate Ledger Cards for each class of PPE.

We recommend that Management designate a specific personnel to work on the transfer of titles of land in the name of the Municipality and to appropriate funds to cover the cost of such transfer of titles.

We recommended that Management require the MA to review the validity of unliquidated obligations or payables which are outstanding for more than two to over five years and if found to have no valid claims against the LGU, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus (Government Equity) pursuant to Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 99-004 and DBM and COA Joint Circular No. 99-6.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer (ME), MA and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the requirements of the Building Code and DOLE Department Order in safeguarding the life, health, property and public welfare in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Building Official or the ME to review and conduct inspection of firms/establishments that obtained a Building Permit during the year to determine whether the building is not being occupied or is not being utilized pending issuance of a corresponding Certificate of Occupancy and inform the owners of the firms/establishments in writing, about the result of their review and inspection and require them to apply for Certificate of Occupancy if warranted; and
Require the ME to prepare and submit monthly to the Municipal Assessor the List of Building Permittees and Certificate of Occupancy issued by their Office, in order for the Municipal Assessor to be able also to conduct inspection for the issuance of Tax Declaration on the Building/Structure issued with Certificate of Occupancy.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to use the proposed format by the Audit Team for the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF (Annex E) and henceforth to prepare and submit the said Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the time frame provided and for the LDRRMO to submit the same reports through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to strengthen the processing/pre-audit activities, as part of the Agency’s overall accounting and internal control system and ensure that transactions being processed, paid and recorded are duly supported with complete documentation and/or technical requirements, otherwise these may result in Notices of Suspensions or Disallowances issued by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE require all employees and officials concerned to immediately submit the requested required supporting documents necessary in the technical evaluation and appraisal of the value of lot, and for the judicious review of the transaction by the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management direct the MA to cause the immediate submission of all unsubmitted DVs, particularly the forty DVs totaling ₱1.12 million, including its SDs, for verification and the judicious audit thereof. Henceforth, strictly observe the timelines set for the rendition/submission of accounts to avoid suspensions/ disallowances in audit.

We requested for the Local Chief Executive to direct the GAD Focal Person to submit GAD ARs to the Audit Team for the purpose of determining the judicious use of the GAD budget, towards helping Management in the assessment of the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.

We commended the LCE and the MA for the early submission of the CY 2018 Year-End FS, prepared in conformity with the PPSAS and in accordance with the timelines of the aforementioned regulations, hence, allowing the Audit Team to conduct timely verification of its FS and financial transactions for the preparation of the  CY  2018  Annual  Audit  Report.     We  also  recognized  the  assistance  and
cooperation of all the Officers and personnel of the LGU, thus, allowing the MA to be able to come up with the required Reports.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the yearend Financial Statements of the LGU, together with its supporting schedules and its preparation in conformity with the PPSAS.

We commended Management for the improved submission of all the above- cited financial reports and recommend that Management:
Request the MA to continue the efforts done in the submission of the Monthly TB and Quarterly FS before the deadlines and henceforth to prepare and submit the same, together with the complete Schedules and Annexes, on or before the prescribed timelines in accordance with the abovementioned existing rules and regulations; and
To augment qualified personnel in the Accounting Unit to perform the duties and functions in the Department to be able to generate accounting reports and be able to submit said reports on time.

We commended Management and MA in the early and/or timely submission of	the	disbursement	vouchers	and	payrolls,	official	receipts,	including	the corresponding reports of each month on or before the tenth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management augment qualified personnel in the Accounting Unit to perform the duties and functions in the Department to be able to generate accounting reports and be able to submit said reports on time.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant (MA) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to terminate the high yield/time deposit accounts in the different banks for the implementation of programs/projects/activities that would benefit its constituents;

Direct the MA and MT to pre-terminate the loan accounts with the Land Bank of the Philippines and avoid the payment of interests/penalties; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant to cause the Municipal Accountant to: (23) use the correct account for recording cash advances for travel ; (24) keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee and monitor strictly the liquidation of said cash advances; (25) send within "10 days before the expiration of the 30-day period a written reminder under the signature of the MunicipalMayor enjoining the official/employee concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance, (26) withhold the salary of the official/employee until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated,  should  the concerned official/employee fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period; and (27) verify and analyze the abnormal negative balance of B455.53 and after which cause the adjustment of the affected accounts.
